concepts.title: Port of Ennore and Kattupalli
concepts.description: Port of Ennore and Kattupalli are major ports in Tamil Nadu, India. They are located near Chennai and play a crucial role in the country's maritime trade.

concepts.title: Indian Railways
concepts.description: Indian Railways is one of the largest railway networks in the world, covering over 121,000 km of track and transporting over 8 million passengers daily.

concepts.title: Inland Waterways
concepts.description: Inland Waterways Authority of India (IWAI) is responsible for developing and maintaining inland waterways in India. The National Waterway 1 (Ganga-Bhagirathi-Hooghly) is a major inland waterway in the country.

concepts.title: Telecom Networks
concepts.description: To remember the components of a telecom network, use the mnemonic 'SATELLITE': Switch, Access, Terminal, Exchange, Line, Internet, Terminal, and Equipment.

concepts.title: Telecom Network Components
concepts.description: Students often confuse the components of a telecom network. To avoid this, remember that Switch and Exchange are not the same; Switch is a device that forwards data packets, while Exchange is a device that connects multiple telephone lines.

concepts.title: Length and Speed of Indian Railways
concepts.description: The length of Indian Railways is over 121,000 km, and the average speed of a train is around 45-50 km/h.

concepts.title: Indian Coast Guard
concepts.description: The Indian Coast Guard is a maritime law enforcement agency responsible for enforcing maritime laws, protecting the country's coastline, and assisting in search and rescue operations.

concepts.title: Telecom Revitalization Policy
concepts.description: The Telecom Revitalization Policy 1999 aimed to liberalize the telecom sector, increase competition, and improve services in India.
